**Andrés Jair Rentería Morelo**[[2]](#cite_note-2) (born 6 March 1993), commonly known as **Andrés Rentería**, is a Colombian [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who plays as a [forward](/wiki/Forward_(association_football) "Forward (association football)") for [Jaguares de Córdoba](/wiki/Jaguares_de_C%C3%B3rdoba "Jaguares de Córdoba").

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

### Atlético Nacional and Alianza Petrolera

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Atlético Nacional and Alianza Petrolera")]

Rentería started his career in [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional")'s youth teams, and debuted in the first team on 4 March 2012, in the 1–0 defeat against [Cúcuta Deportivo](/wiki/C%C3%BAcuta_Deportivo "Cúcuta Deportivo"), coming on as an 81st-minute substitute for [Diego Álvarez](/wiki/Diego_%C3%81lvarez_(footballer) "Diego Álvarez (footballer)"). He began his career as a [centre forward](/wiki/Forward_(association_football) "Forward (association football)"), but eventually adopted its current position of [winger](/wiki/Midfielder#Winger "Midfielder").

On 30 June, he was loaned to [Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B") club [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera "Alianza Petrolera").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He debuted for the team on 28 July, in the 1–4 win against [Uniautónoma](/wiki/Uniaut%C3%B3noma_FC "Uniautónoma FC"), where he scored the 4 goals of his team.[[4]](#cite_note-4) He would score 14 goals in 21 matches, becoming the season's top scorer,[[5]](#cite_note-5) and helping to Alianza Petrolera achieve promotion to the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A"), for the first time in their history.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Santos Laguna

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Santos Laguna")]

On 11 December 2012, Rentería was sold to [Liga MX](/wiki/Liga_MX "Liga MX") club [Santos Laguna](/wiki/Club_Santos_Laguna "Club Santos Laguna").[[7]](#cite_note-7) He debuted for the team on 20 January, in the 2–1 defeat against [Puebla](/wiki/Puebla_F.C. "Puebla F.C.").[[8]](#cite_note-8) Rentería would score his first goal on 12 May, in the 1–3 win against [Atlas](/wiki/Club_Atlas "Club Atlas").[[9]](#cite_note-9) On 5 November 2014, Santos won the [Copa MX](/wiki/Copa_MX "Copa MX") beating Puebla 4–2 in a penalty shootout, after a 2–2 draw. Rentería scored the opening goal of the match.[[10]](#cite_note-10) On 21 July 2015, Santos Laguna won the [2015 Clausura](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Liga_MX_season "2014–15 Liga MX season") of the Liga MX.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Querétaro

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Querétaro")]

On 8 June 2016, Rentería joined [Querétaro](/wiki/Quer%C3%A9taro_F.C. "Querétaro F.C.") on a permanent transfer.[[12]](#cite_note-12) On 2 November 2016, Querétaro beat [Chivas](/wiki/C.D._Guadalajara "C.D. Guadalajara") 3–2 in a penalty shootout to win Copa MX,[[13]](#cite_note-13) despite receiving a red card 7 minutes after being subbed in.[[14]](#cite_note-14) On 16 July 2017, his team won the [2017 Supercopa MX](/wiki/2017_Supercopa_MX "2017 Supercopa MX").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Return to Atlético Nacional

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Return to Atlético Nacional")]

On 22 July 2017, Rentería returned to Colombia rejoining [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional") on a one-year loan, with an option to buy for a $4 million fee.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

### Cruz Azul

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Cruz Azul")]

On 16 June 2018, [Cruz Azul](/wiki/Cruz_Azul "Cruz Azul") announced the signing of Rentería.[[17]](#cite_note-17) On July 21, 2018, he made his debut with Cruz Azul and scored his first goal in the 80th minute in a 3–0 victory against Puebla.[[18]](#cite_note-18) In November 2018, he won Copa MX when Cruz Azul beat [Monterrey](/wiki/C.F._Monterrey "C.F. Monterrey") 2–0 in the final.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

#### Loans

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Loans")]

On 28 January 2019 it was confirmed, that he would spend the rest of 2019 on loan at [San Lorenzo](/wiki/San_Lorenzo_de_Almagro "San Lorenzo de Almagro"),[[20]](#cite_note-20) with an option to join permanently for a $3 million fee.[[21]](#cite_note-21) His loan was terminated on 14 May 2019, due to his poor performance.[[22]](#cite_note-22) On 18 July 2019, he joined [Rionegro Águilas](/wiki/%C3%81guilas_Doradas_Rionegro "Águilas Doradas Rionegro") on loan until the end of the 2019 season.[[23]](#cite_note-23) On 18 November, he returned to Cruz Azul after a disappointing loan spell.[[24]](#cite_note-24) After failing to loan him with an option to buy,[[25]](#cite_note-25) he returned on loan to Rionegro.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

### Later career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Later career")]

On 11 January 2021, Rentería joined [Independiente Santa Fe](/wiki/Independiente_Santa_Fe "Independiente Santa Fe").[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28) On 30 July 2021, he joined [Jaguares de Córdoba](/wiki/Jaguares_de_C%C3%B3rdoba "Jaguares de Córdoba").[[29]](#cite_note-29) On 27 June 2022, he joined [Deportes Tolima](/wiki/Deportes_Tolima "Deportes Tolima").[[30]](#cite_note-30) On 14 July 2023, he rejoined [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera_F.C. "Alianza Petrolera F.C.").[[31]](#cite_note-31)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: International career")]

Rentería was called for the [Colombia U20](/wiki/Colombia_national_under-20_football_team "Colombia national under-20 football team") squad of the [2013 Toulon Tournament](/wiki/2013_Toulon_Tournament "2013 Toulon Tournament"). He played 4 matches, and scored a goal in the 80th minute of the 1–3 win against [France U20](/wiki/France_national_football_team "France national football team").

He also was called for the squad that played the [2013 FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/2013_FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "2013 FIFA U-20 World Cup") in Turkey, where he played 3 matches, and scored a goal in the 21st minute of the 0–3 win against [El Salvador U-20](/wiki/El_Salvador_national_under-20_football_team "El Salvador national under-20 football team").

On 22 March 2015 he was called up by [Colombia national team](/wiki/Colombia_national_football_team "Colombia national football team") for the [friendlies](/wiki/Exhibition_match "Exhibition match") against [Bahrain](/wiki/Bahrain_national_football_team "Bahrain national football team") and [Kuwait](/wiki/Kuwait_national_football_team "Kuwait national football team"). He made his international debut four days later, coming on as a second-half substitute for [Radamel Falcao García](/wiki/Radamel_Falcao_Garc%C3%ADa "Radamel Falcao García"), assisting [Johan Mojica](/wiki/Johan_Mojica "Johan Mojica") in the 79th minute and scoring in the 82nd, assisted by Mojica himself in a 6–0 win.

On 25 March 2016, Rentería made his debut for the [Colombia Olympic football team](/wiki/Colombia_Olympic_football_team "Colombia Olympic football team") in the [2016 Summer Olympics CONCACAF–CONMEBOL play-off](/wiki/Football_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics_CONCACAF%E2%80%93CONMEBOL_play-off "Football at the 2016 Summer Olympics CONCACAF–CONMEBOL play-off"). Subsequently, the team qualified to the [2016 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Football_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics "Football at the 2016 Summer Olympics"). He was left out of the roster for the [2016 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Football_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics "Football at the 2016 Summer Olympics") due to injury.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andr%C3%A9s_Renter%C3%ADa&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Personal life")]

He is a naturalized citizen of Mexico.[[33]](#cite_note-33)[[34]](#cite_note-34)
